raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The general request for feedback implies an evaluation of how well the core contribution is framed and communicated.",
    "R1.2": "The request invites scrutiny of the logical flow from the problem statement to the conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "Assessing the work requires checking if the reported quantitative results support the qualitative claims.",
    "R1.4": "A core part of evaluating a research abstract is to assess the strength and credibility of its novelty claim.",
    "R1.6": "The open-ended feedback request includes an implicit check for clarity, conciseness, and overall writing quality.",
    "R1.7": "The delegation requires an assessment of the work's positioning within its field, including the 'first to apply' claim.",
    "RX.1": "The request to evaluate the work necessitates checking if uncertainties, especially regarding the performance trade-off, are properly disclosed.",
    "RX.2": "A primary responsibility when reviewing any research is to ensure that the claims made are not exaggerated relative to the evidence provided.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must constrain its feedback to the provided artifact, as implied by the general but bounded nature of the request.",
    "RX.4": "Given the artifact's application to flood-risk, a critical part of the review is to consider the potential real-world consequences of the proposed accuracy-cost trade-off.",
    "RX.5": "The agent's feedback must be grounded entirely in the text of the artifact to be traceable and valid."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: The general request for feedback implies an evaluation of how well the core contribution is framed and communicated.
rationale.R1.2: The request invites scrutiny of the logical flow from the problem statement to the conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: Assessing the work requires checking if the reported quantitative results support the qualitative claims.
rationale.R1.4: A core part of evaluating a research abstract is to assess the strength and credibility of its novelty claim.
rationale.R1.6: The open-ended feedback request includes an implicit check for clarity, conciseness, and overall writing quality.
rationale.R1.7: The delegation requires an assessment of the work's positioning within its field, including the 'first to apply' claim.
rationale.RX.1: The request to evaluate the work necessitates checking if uncertainties, especially regarding the performance trade-off, are properly disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: A primary responsibility when reviewing any research is to ensure that the claims made are not exaggerated relative to the evidence provided.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must constrain its feedback to the provided artifact, as implied by the general but bounded nature of the request.
rationale.RX.4: Given the artifact's application to flood-risk, a critical part of the review is to consider the potential real-world consequences of the proposed accuracy-cost trade-off.
rationale.RX.5: The agent's feedback must be grounded entirely in the text of the artifact to be traceable and valid.
